# criblio_pack_vars (Resource)

PackVars Resource

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "criblio_pack_vars" "my_packvars" {
  description = "my_description"
  group_id    = "default"
  id          = "my_id"
  lib         = "custom"
  pack        = criblio_pack.vars_pack.id
  tags        = "my_tags"
  type        = "object"
  value       = "my_value"
}

resource "criblio_pack" "vars_pack" {
  id           = "pack-with-vars"
  group_id     = "default"
  description  = "Pack from source"
  disabled     = true
  display_name = "Pack from source"
  source       = "file:/opt/cribl_data/failover/groups/default/default/HelloPacks"
  version      = "1.0.0"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tools/codegen -->
## Schema

### Required

- `group_id` (String) Worker group ID.
- `id` (String) Global variable name
- `pack` (String) The <code>id</code> of the Pack.
- `type` (String)

### Optional

- `args` (Attributes List) Argument definitions for expression-type variables. Each item has type and name (e.g. for (val / 1073741824).toFixed(precision || 5)). (see [below for nested schema](#nestedatt--args))
- `description` (String)
- `lib` (String)
- `tags` (String)
- `value` (String)

<a id="nestedatt--args"></a>
### Nested Schema for `args`

Required:

- `type` (String) Argument type (e.g. number, string)
- `name` (String) Argument name

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, the [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) can be used with the `id` attribute, for example:

```terraform
import {
  to = criblio_pack_vars.my_criblio_pack_vars
  id = jsonencode({
    group_id = "Cribl"
    id       = "ENV"
    pack     = "example-pack"
  })
}
```

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
terraform import criblio_pack_vars.my_criblio_pack_vars '{"group_id": "Cribl", "id": "ENV", "pack": "example-pack"}'
```
